learning_outcomes.description: Analyse key sites through which gender identities emerge.
Examine media representations of bodies, genders and sexualities through intersectional gendered theoretical frameworks.
An interdisciplinary approach to media analysis.
Research evidence and articulate arguments about culture, media, and gender.
The ability to think critically and analytically about gender representation in the world around them, and to articulate those thoughts in writing.

assessments.task_description:
Tutorials commence in Week 2 of semester. The participation mark will be determined by considering a student’s overall participation in the tutorial series. The quality of a student’s contribution (not mere frequency of attendance) will be the main criteria.

To ensure you do well in tutorials you should always:

  • Do the readings.
  • Think about the broader implications of the topic in terms of theory and real-world applications.
  • Contribute to discussions meaningfully.
  • Engage respectfully with others.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
You will provide a research proposal outlining your intended approach to your Video Essay. You may choose any aspect of gender and contemporary culture to focus on. This proposal will include a research question/s, argument, and main points you intend to use to support your argument and answer your research question. You will also provide an annotated bibliography of at least 5 scholarly sources you will use in your Video Essay, providing analysis of the sources in relation to your chosen topic. More information on this will be made available in lectures and/or tutorials.

Referencing Syle: Students may choose whichever referencing style they are comfortable with, as long as it remains consistent with the UQ Library style guide: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing. All referencing requires specific page numbers.

Word Limits: A final word count that is within ±10% of the set length (500 words) is acceptable. Word count does not include any referencing or bibliography; however, discursive footnotes are included in the word count. A word count that is outside these 10% will receive a proportionate penalty and will be graded against the grading criteria.

AI: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You will deliver a Video Essay that addresses an aspect of gender and contemporary culture of your choosing. You will need to have your topic approved through your Video Essay Proposal assessment. In your final Video Essay, you will need to:

  • Outline your research question/s
  • Clearly articulate your argument in response
  • Support your argument through a clear, logical discussion
  • Use at least 5 scholarly sources in your transcript to support your argument and discussion
  • Demonstrate some creativity in visuals and/or sounds in your final Video Essay production


More information about the task will be made available to you via Blackboard.

Referencing Syle: Students may choose whichever referencing style they are comfortable with, as long as it remains consistent with the UQ Library style guide: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing. All referencing requires specific page numbers.

AI: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will need to upload a video file and a fully referenced transcript of your Video Essay.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
More information about the exam will be discussed in the Week 13 Lecture. The purpose of the exam is to identify the knowledge you have acquired and your grasp of the broader themes from the course.

AI: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
